items.text:
Subject: Trading Thursday shift at the Library Café

Hi Jordan,

I hope you had a good weekend. I'm writing to ask whether you could cover my Thursday evening shift at the Library Café this week. My research seminar got rescheduled to that same time slot, and I won't be able to make it in.

If you can take the shift from 5:00 to 9:00 PM on Thursday, March 21, I'm happy to cover any shift of yours during finals week — including the busy Saturday 11:00 AM to 3:00 PM block, which pays the $2 weekend bonus. I'll also bring you coffee on me as thanks.

Please let me know by Wednesday afternoon so I can update Ms. Tanaka if neither of us is available. Apologies for the late notice, and thanks for considering it.

Best,
Riley

format_metadata.subject: Trading Thursday shift at the Library Café

questions.stem: According to the email, what time is Riley's Thursday shift?
questions.explanation: The email states the shift runs 'from 5:00 to 9:00 PM on Thursday, March 21.'

questions.stem: The word 'cover' in the email is closest in meaning to
questions.explanation: Riley asks Jordan whether he could 'cover my Thursday evening shift' — that is, work the shift in Riley's place.

questions.stem: What can be inferred from Riley's email?

options.A: Riley plans to leave the Library Café position soon
options.B: Riley regularly works double shifts on weekend days
options.C: Riley considers Jordan a reliable colleague
options.D: Riley has not yet told the manager about the conflict

questions.explanation: Riley directly asks Jordan to cover and offers to reciprocate during finals week, implying confidence that Jordan is dependable.

items.text:
Subject: Overdue notice for 'Patterns of Ancient Trade'

Dear Ms. Caldwell,

This is a reminder from the University Library that the book 'Patterns of Ancient Trade' borrowed from the Anthropology section is now nine days overdue. The original due date was Tuesday, March 5.

To avoid additional fees, please return the book to the circulation desk on the second floor of Henderson Library by Saturday, March 16. Current charges stand at $9.00, and they will continue to accrue at $1.00 per day until the book is returned or paid for.

If you have already returned the book or believe this notice was sent in error, please reply to this message or visit the circulation desk between 8:00 AM and 6:00 PM, Monday through Friday. Our staff can review your account and clear any mistake.

Thank you for your prompt attention.

Best regards,
The University Library Team

format_metadata.subject: Overdue notice for 'Patterns of Ancient Trade'

questions.stem: According to the email, what is the deadline for returning the book without additional fees?
questions.explanation: The email instructs Ms. Caldwell to return the book 'by Saturday, March 16' to avoid additional fees.

questions.stem: What is the main purpose of this email?

options.A: To inform Ms. Caldwell that the library is closing for renovations
options.B: To remind Ms. Caldwell that a borrowed book is overdue
options.C: To announce a new policy for circulating reference materials
options.D: To request that Ms. Caldwell renew her library membership

questions.explanation: The subject line ('Overdue notice') and the body explicitly remind Ms. Caldwell about a book that is nine days overdue.

questions.stem: What can be inferred about the daily late fee?

options.A: The library waives the daily fees if requested in writing
options.B: The fee will continue to grow until the book is returned
options.C: The fee applies only to undergraduate university students
options.D: The fee can be paid only by credit card at checkout

questions.explanation: The email notes that charges 'will continue to accrue at $1.00 per day until the book is returned,' so the fee keeps growing until the book is returned.
